title: The Insulin Price Gap
alt_text: Data visualization: insulin retail prices by country — US far above all peers
slides:
Germany pays $11 for a vial of insulin. The US pays $98.70.

Same drug. Same molecule. Often the same manufacturer.

Every country below the US has national drug price negotiation. The US doesn't.

That's the mechanism. Not R&D costs. Not manufacturing. Just: who has the power to set the price.

Insulin's patent sold for $1 in 1921. Frederick Banting refused to profit from something people need to survive.

About 9 million Americans now ration their doses.
